summary:
• De-escalating US-Iran tensions and peace deal signals are weighing on oil prices, reducing immediate geopolitical supply risk from potential Strait of Hormuz disruptions despite periodic tension spikes.

• Russian crude output remains at year-lows due to sustained Ukrainian strikes on infrastructure, constraining non-OPEC supply and supporting limited upside to global crude availability.

• Western Hemisphere production is being bolstered by new project acquisitions (APA's Savant Alaska, Woodside's Browse JV stake) while downstream LNG infrastructure investments signal confidence in long-term Americas energy exports despite near-term pricing pressures.
